name: Kentucky Consumer Data Protection Act
summary: The Kentucky Consumer Data Protection Act provides consumer data privacy rights for Kentucky residents. It applies to entities conducting business in Kentucky or targeting its residents that control or process personal data of at least 100,000 consumers, or 25,000 consumers if deriving more than 50% of gross revenue from the sale of personal data.
keyRequirements: Right to confirm whether a controller is processing personal data and to access that data
Right to correct inaccuracies in personal data
Right to delete personal data provided by or obtained about the consumer
Right to obtain a copy of personal data in a portable and readily usable format
Right to opt out of sale of personal data, targeted advertising, and profiling
Consent required before processing sensitive data such as racial, health, and biometric data
Controllers must conduct data protection assessments for high-risk processing activities
penalties: Enforced exclusively by the Kentucky Attorney General. Civil penalties up to $7,500 per violation. 30-day cure period for violations; no private right of action.
